verses.text: Saul, 'breathing out threatenings,' seeks letters to bind the disciples in Damascus. Raging foe. The fiercest enemy is about to be changed.
Saul, 'breathing out threatenings,' seeks letters to bind the disciples in Damascus. Raging foe. The fiercest enemy is about to be changed.
A light shines; 'Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? I am Jesus'; trembling, Lord, what wilt thou have me do? Stopped in his tracks. The persecutor meets the risen Christ.
A light shines; 'Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? I am Jesus'; trembling, Lord, what wilt thou have me do? Stopped in his tracks. The persecutor meets the risen Christ.
A light shines; 'Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? I am Jesus'; trembling, Lord, what wilt thou have me do? Stopped in his tracks. The persecutor meets the risen Christ.
A light shines; 'Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? I am Jesus'; trembling, Lord, what wilt thou have me do? Stopped in his tracks. The persecutor meets the risen Christ.
Blinded, he is led to Damascus and fasts three days. Humbled. The proud man is laid low to be made new.
Blinded, he is led to Damascus and fasts three days. Humbled. The proud man is laid low to be made new.
Blinded, he is led to Damascus and fasts three days. Humbled. The proud man is laid low to be made new.
The Lord sends Ananias to Saul, who is praying. Praying foe. The changed heart turns at once to God.
The Lord sends Ananias to Saul, who is praying. Praying foe. The changed heart turns at once to God.
The Lord sends Ananias to Saul, who is praying. Praying foe. The changed heart turns at once to God.
Ananias fears, but Saul is 'a chosen vessel' to bear His name before Gentiles and kings. Chosen instrument. God's grace makes the enemy His apostle.
Ananias fears, but Saul is 'a chosen vessel' to bear His name before Gentiles and kings. Chosen instrument. God's grace makes the enemy His apostle.
Ananias fears, but Saul is 'a chosen vessel' to bear His name before Gentiles and kings. Chosen instrument. God's grace makes the enemy His apostle.
Ananias fears, but Saul is 'a chosen vessel' to bear His name before Gentiles and kings. Chosen instrument. God's grace makes the enemy His apostle.
Ananias lays hands on him; scales fall, he sees, is baptized, and is strengthened. Sight restored. He rises a new man in Christ.
Ananias lays hands on him; scales fall, he sees, is baptized, and is strengthened. Sight restored. He rises a new man in Christ.
Ananias lays hands on him; scales fall, he sees, is baptized, and is strengthened. Sight restored. He rises a new man in Christ.
Straightway he preaches Christ; all are amazed; he confounds the Jews. Turned about. The persecutor now proclaims the faith.
Straightway he preaches Christ; all are amazed; he confounds the Jews. Turned about. The persecutor now proclaims the faith.
Straightway he preaches Christ; all are amazed; he confounds the Jews. Turned about. The persecutor now proclaims the faith.
The Jews plot to kill him; the disciples let him down the wall in a basket. Hunted now. He tastes the persecution he once dealt.
The Jews plot to kill him; the disciples let him down the wall in a basket. Hunted now. He tastes the persecution he once dealt.
The Jews plot to kill him; the disciples let him down the wall in a basket. Hunted now. He tastes the persecution he once dealt.
At Jerusalem the disciples fear him; Barnabas brings him to the apostles. A friend's help. Barnabas vouches for the new convert.
At Jerusalem the disciples fear him; Barnabas brings him to the apostles. A friend's help. Barnabas vouches for the new convert.
At Jerusalem the disciples fear him; Barnabas brings him to the apostles. A friend's help. Barnabas vouches for the new convert.
He disputes; they seek to slay him; sent to Tarsus; 'then had the churches rest.' A breathing space. God grants the church a season of peace.
He disputes; they seek to slay him; sent to Tarsus; 'then had the churches rest.' A breathing space. God grants the church a season of peace.
He disputes; they seek to slay him; sent to Tarsus; 'then had the churches rest.' A breathing space. God grants the church a season of peace.
Peter heals Aeneas of the palsy at Lydda, and all turn to the Lord. Mercy and fruit. The miracle turns many to Christ.
Peter heals Aeneas of the palsy at Lydda, and all turn to the Lord. Mercy and fruit. The miracle turns many to Christ.
Peter heals Aeneas of the palsy at Lydda, and all turn to the Lord. Mercy and fruit. The miracle turns many to Christ.
Peter heals Aeneas of the palsy at Lydda, and all turn to the Lord. Mercy and fruit. The miracle turns many to Christ.
Dorcas, full of good works, dies; they send for Peter, showing the coats she made. Mourned saint. Her kindness is her lasting memorial.
Dorcas, full of good works, dies; they send for Peter, showing the coats she made. Mourned saint. Her kindness is her lasting memorial.
Dorcas, full of good works, dies; they send for Peter, showing the coats she made. Mourned saint. Her kindness is her lasting memorial.
Dorcas, full of good works, dies; they send for Peter, showing the coats she made. Mourned saint. Her kindness is her lasting memorial.
Peter prays, 'Tabitha, arise'; he presents her alive; many believe. Life restored. The raising of Dorcas brings many to faith.
